steps.title: Recognize the indirect question structure
steps.content: The verb asked followed by what signals an indirect (embedded) question — a question folded into a statement. Indirect questions must use statement word order (subject before verb) and the sentence ends with the outer sentence's punctuation, not a question mark or exclamation mark.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm
steps.content:
• "was her favorite book!": Inverted (question) word order and an exclamation mark — both wrong for an embedded clause. ✗
  • "her favorite book was.": Subject (her favorite book) before verb (was) — correct statement order — and a period ends the declarative sentence. ✓
  • "her favorite book was?": Word order is correct, but a question mark punctuates the whole sentence as a question, which it is not. ✗
  • "was her favorite book?": Inverted word order and a question mark — both wrong. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
